title: Gonzalagunia asperula
taxonomy: I can find information about Gonzalagunia asperula's classification. Gonzalagunia asperula is classified as a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. Gonzalagunia asperula is further classified as a member of the tribe Psychotrieae within the Rubiaceae family. Within the tribe, it is placed in the subtribe Psychotriinae.

intro: Gonzalagunia asperula, commonly known as "Wild Gonzalagunia" or "Wild Asperula", is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its purported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gonzalagunia asperula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
